exercises.title: Props
exercises.description: Create a Greeting component that accepts a name prop and renders Hello, {name}! inside a p tag. Render it with name "Alice".
exercises.solutionCode: import { useState } from 'react'; export default function Counter() { const [count, setCount] = useState(0); return <button onClick={() => setCount(count + 1)}>Count: {count}</button>; }

exercises.title: Event Handling
exercises.description: Create a ClickCounter component with a button. Use React.useState to track clicks. Display Clicked: X times.
exercises.hint: const [count, setCount] = React.useState(0); then onClick={() => setCount(count + 1)}
exercises.solutionCode: export default function Button({ onClick, children }) { return <button onClick={onClick}>{children}</button>; }

exercises.title: useState Hook
exercises.description: Create a NameInput component with a text input. Use useState to display what the user types in real-time below the input as Hello, {name}!.
exercises.hint: Use onChange={(e) => setName(e.target.value)} on the input.
exercises.solutionCode: import { useState } from 'react'; export default function Form() { const [text, setText] = useState(''); return <input value={text} onChange={(e) => setText(e.target.value)} />; }

exercises.title: Component Composition
exercises.description: Create a Card component that takes title and children props. Render a UserCard component that uses Card with title "User Profile" and content "Name: Alice".
exercises.hint: Card renders {props.title} in h2 and {props.children} for nested content.
exercises.solutionCode:
import { createContext, useContext } from 'react'; const ThemeContext = createContext('light'); export default function App() { return ; } function Child() { const theme = useContext(ThemeContext); return
{theme}
; }

exercises.title: useEffect Hook
exercises.description: Create a Timer component that uses useEffect to update a counter every second. Display Seconds: X.
exercises.hint: Use useEffect with setInterval and return a cleanup function.
exercises.solutionCode: import { useRef } from 'react'; export default function FocusInput() { const inputRef = useRef(null); return <><input ref={inputRef} /><button onClick={() => inputRef.current.focus()}>Focus</button></>; }

exercises.title: Todo List App
exercises.description: Build a simple TodoApp with: an input to type a task, an "Add" button, and a list that displays added tasks. Use useState for both the input value and the todo array.
exercises.hint: Use two useState hooks: one for todos array and one for current input text.
exercises.solutionCode: import { useReducer } from 'react'; function reducer(state, action) { return action.type === 'inc' ? state + 1 : state; } export default function Counter() { const [count, dispatch] = useReducer(reducer, 0); return <button onClick={() => dispatch({type: 'inc'})}>{count}</button>; }
